inputs.titre: Consécration de la cathédrale de Santiago de Compostela

reason: The image depicts the interior of a large cathedral during what appears to be a consecration ceremony. The architectural style is generally consistent with the Romanesque/Gothic transitional period, though some elements appear slightly later. The clothing of the attendees is mostly plausible, consisting of tunics and cloaks. Some of the hairstyles and the cut of some clothing, particularly at the back of the crowd, give me modern vibes. The candles and stained-glass windows are appropriate. However, the portraits adorning the walls are not entirely in keeping with the styles of the early 13th century; panel paintings were common but generally were altarpieces and some of the styles are later Renaissance-inspired portraits (especially those depicting busts). Given some doubts about the clothing and painting styles, the image is marked as 'valid' but the score is not perfect.
